name: Comeketo Wedding Experience — Sales Deck (Brand & Voice)
subject: Wedding vertical sales deck "Beyond Catering: Your Partner in Creating the Perfect Day". Primary source for brand voice, positioning claims, signature offers, and closing choreography for the wedding segment. Intended as upstream feedstock for Rodbot voice/character work.
source_files: The Comeketo Wedding Experience_ Beyond Catering 2.pptx.txt

headline_positioning.lead_line: Beyond Catering: Your Partner in Creating the Perfect Day
headline_positioning.sub_line: Why 750+ Couples Trusted Comeketo to Lead the Way
headline_positioning.plain_english_pitch: We've helped 750+ couples create their perfect day. We turn planning stress into smooth sailing by acting as a Central Research Hub for vetted professionals — rentals, decor, MC, photo booth, souvenirs — with a 2-year price lock and an integrity promise (no kickbacks).
headline_positioning.voice_tone: Warm, reassuring, confident. Uses the couple's emotional stakes (stress, overwhelm, decision fatigue) as the entry point, then pivots to Comeketo as the calm, experienced hand that takes over.
anchor_phrases: Trust is what matters in this business

messaging_pillars.pillar: Partnership Pile-Up vs. The Comeketo Way
messaging_pillars.claim_form: Comparison table: Traditional vs Comeketo across Research / Coordination / Stress.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Name the problem the prospect is living in (decision fatigue) before offering the relief. Positions Comeketo as a process/operations upgrade, not just a vendor.

offers_bundled.detail: High-quality chairs and tables through exclusive partners.
Professional decor services that transform any venue.
Expert MC services to guide guests and keep the flow perfect.
High-end, editorial-style photo experience for guests.

messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Expands the wallet from 'catering' to 'full event ecosystem' without asking the couple to manage more vendors.

messaging_pillars.claim_form: Your event doesn't start when guests arrive — it starts 7 days prior in our logistics hub. Choreographed loading of the specialized fleet, every item secured.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Shows invisible labor. Converts behind-the-scenes discipline into a trust signal.
messaging_pillars.note_on_copy_inconsistency: Headline says 'Week Before' / '7 days prior' but the speaker-notes paragraph says '24 hours before any guest arrives.' Deck has a 7-day vs 24-hour inconsistency; flag for copy fix.

proof_points: Fleet of specialized vehicles — scale + reliability + punctuality, any size event.
Dedicated packing list per party; double-check every table, chair, utensil.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Scale signaling. Answers the unspoken 'can they actually pull this off?' question.

messaging_pillars.pillar: Financial Peace of Mind & Integrity Guarantee

promises.detail: Secure today's rates for up to 24 months — protection from inflation.
promises.note: Slide header and body say '1-Year Price Lock' in one place and '2-Year Price Lock' in another. Reconcile — 24-month number is consistent with 2-year framing, so probably '2-Year' is the intended copy.

promises.detail: We refer partners because they are the best, not because they pay us. No kickbacks, no hidden fees.

messaging_pillars.signature_quote: Trust is what matters in this business.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Neutralizes two common objections — price uncertainty and 'are these real recommendations or paid placements?' — in one slide.
Loaded present-moment reward — converts attendance at a tasting into meaningful savings to create a booking trigger. Combined with scarcity (slide 8) this is the closing lever.
messaging_pillars.math_note: 150 + 350 + 650 = $1,150 from listed free items; the advertised $2,350+ total implies the partner discounts are valued around $1,200+. That gap should be closable with a specific partner-discount breakdown when asked.

pressure_points.claim: Calendar fills 12–18 months in advance; popular dates often secured years ahead.
Today's rates are only guaranteed upon booking.
The $2,350+ in upgrades/discounts is a one-time offer for today's attendees only.
The biggest regret we hear is waiting too long.

messaging_pillars.close_line: Lock in your peace of mind before you leave.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Four stacked scarcity angles. Each attacks a different hesitation (date / price / bonus / regret).

path_a.label: The DIY Path
costs: Risk unexpected price hikes and inflation
Carry the full weight of wedding stress

path_b.label: Comeketo Auto-Pilot
wins: Access our vetted, integrity-based partner network
Relax while we handle the heavy lifting

messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Forced dichotomy. Converts 'should I book?' into 'which path?' — a softer yes.

three_step_close: Review your custom quote with our team.
Secure your date with a deposit today.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Small, numbered steps make the deposit feel procedural rather than terrifying.

messaging_pillars.claim_form: Custom-created Souvenir Packages extend the joy of the wedding day beyond the reception.
messaging_pillars.underlying_move: Ends on warmth rather than urgency. Re-opens upsell space for a takeaway product.

750_plus_couples.verify: Confirm against the real book — is this approximate, exact, or a round-up?

10_plus_years.verify: Confirm founding year; confirm which awards.

price_lock.verify: Confirm current policy, exact term window, what voids it.

integrity_promise.claim: No kickbacks, no hidden fees from referred partners.
integrity_promise.verify: Operationally true? If any referral fees exist, the copy needs rework before Rodbot repeats it.

booking_window.claim: Calendar fills 12–18 months in advance; popular dates secured years ahead.
booking_window.verify: Check against actual booking horizon data; this is a closing lever so it should be current.

tasting_bonus_total.verify: Partner-discount math needs a stated breakdown.

rodbot_voice_hooks.why_this_file_matters_for_rodbot: Rodbot is the AI persona that writes/delivers content on behalf of Comeketo. This deck is the clearest pre-existing articulation of the brand's first-person voice in a customer-facing moment (the sales tasting). Use it as a style reference rather than a template — Rodbot should be fluent in this voice but not constrained to parroting it verbatim.
voice_signature: Warm, low-pressure, takes the prospect's stress seriously before offering the fix.
Problem-frame before solution-frame on every pillar. The customer's pain is named first.
Plural first-person ('we') owns responsibility; 'you' addresses the couple directly. Never third-person distance.
Short, confident claims punctuated with specific numbers (750, 10+, 24-month, $2,350, 12–18 months, 40+ hours, 100+ hours).
Binary closes: DIY vs Auto-Pilot / Partnership Pile-Up vs Comeketo Way.
do_use: Name the stress, then dissolve it.
Numbers over adjectives — prefer '750 couples' to 'many couples', '24 months' to 'a long time'.
Binary-choice framing when pushing a decision.
'We' language for ownership; 'you' language for outcomes.
do_not_use: Pure superlatives without a number behind them ('the best', 'amazing', 'incredible').
Price-lock / integrity / 750-couples claims without verifying the current number.
Scarcity language outside of legitimate constraints (don't invent waitlist pressure).
The tasting-bonus stack as fixed dollar figures in writing until the breakdown is confirmed.
open_questions_for_rodbot_identity_pass: Should Rodbot ever sign messages as Rodrigo, or always as Comeketo/team? The deck is 'we'; Rodbot personality needs a rule here.
Tolerance for direct closes — the deck pushes urgency firmly; is that Rodbot's register in 1:1 replies or only in group/sales settings?
Portuguese/Brazilian brand anchoring does not appear in this deck. Voice policy on bilingual or culturally-anchored phrases needs its own pass (welcome_deck + sales playbook likely carry that layer).

copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.issue: 7-days-prior vs 24-hours-prior inconsistency on slide 4 (Precision in Motion).
copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.where: Headline says 'Week Before' and body says '7 days prior'; speaker notes say '24 hours before any guest arrives.'
copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.fix: Pick one and align both. Operationally most likely 7-day load-out process with 24-hour final-check — that framing would let both live.

copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.issue: 1-Year vs 2-Year Price Lock inconsistency on slide 6.
copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.where: Header lists '1-Year Price Lock' and body says 'Secure today's rates for up to 24 months', speaker notes say '2-Year Price Lock.'
copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.fix: Confirm actual policy. If 24 months is correct, update header to 'Up to 2-Year Price Lock' everywhere.

copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.where: Listed items sum to $1,150 but advertised total is $2,350+.
copy_issues_and_fixes_needed.fix: Add one line itemizing partner-discount values so the $2,350 number reconciles — otherwise the prospect can spot the gap.

pillars_that_map_to_other_bedrock_files.depends_on: venue_partners.json (for venue-side rentals/decor availability) + a future partner_network.json once the Partnership Program .md is indexed.
pillars_that_map_to_other_bedrock_files.why: Claims around 'exclusive partners' and 'vetted professionals' need a real underlying list for Rodbot to cite without hallucinating.

pillars_that_map_to_other_bedrock_files.depends_on: staff_labor_roster.json (captains/crew) + kitchen schedule + weekly prep list
pillars_that_map_to_other_bedrock_files.why: The 'choreographed dance' claim lives or dies on actual logistics discipline. Any drift in scheduling hygiene cheapens the pitch.
If the $2,350 stack is changed, the conversion tracker needs the same version number so A/B decisions are legible.

pillars_that_map_to_other_bedrock_files.depends_on: Partnership Program .md (rev share / referral compensation structure)
pillars_that_map_to_other_bedrock_files.why: If any partner compensation moves money, the 'no kickbacks' language needs legal review or rework. This is the single riskiest claim in the deck.

notes: This deck is the wedding-vertical version. Rodrigo's 'Welcome To Comeketo' deck (31KB, not yet indexed) is almost certainly the founder/origin-story companion piece — the pair will form the main brand-voice corpus.

Deck copy appears to be dual-use: visual slide text + speaker-notes paragraphs. When pulling quotes for Rodbot training, the speaker-notes are the authentic spoken voice; the slide bullets are the written shorthand.
750-couples figure and 10-year-expertise figure appear standalone on this file. Both should be verified against the source of truth (founder record / bookings export) before Rodbot ever repeats them.

sources.diff: Initial index from The Comeketo Wedding Experience_ Beyond Catering 2.pptx.txt — 11 slides, 9 messaging pillars extracted, copy inconsistencies flagged, Rodbot voice hooks articulated.
